steps.title: The Immediate Transportation Crisis
steps.image_description: busy city highway with cars and trucks

frames.speech: Imagine waking up, and every single car, bus, and plane on Earth just stops working.
Petroleum, or oil, is a fossil fuel—that is, ancient plants turned into liquid energy—that powers almost all our engines.
Think of oil like the world's blood; without it, the heart of our travel stops beating.
Without trucks, food cannot get from farms to stores. This is a supply chain—the path things take to reach you—breaking down.
Look at the screen. Normally, trucks carry food from farms to your grocery store, but without fuel, that line is cut.

frames.speech: If trucks stopped moving tomorrow, what would happen to the snacks in your pantry?
Actually, they would vanish! Without gas, stores would run out of fresh food in just a few days.
Did you know plastic is made from oil? It is a synthetic polymer—a man-made material—that is basically oil in solid form.
Imagine your favorite plastic action figure or gaming controller just deciding to quit and disappear!
Look around your room right now. Almost every plastic toy, bottle, and case would be gone if the oil stopped.
Many power plants burn fossil fuels to make electricity. Without oil, our lights, computers, and video games might flicker off.
This means no charging your tablet, no heater in the winter, and definitely no late-night gaming sessions.

steps.title: Transitioning to Sustainable Alternatives

frames.speech: This is why we are switching to renewable energy—power from sun, wind, and water—that never runs out.
But is it actually possible to build a whole world without using a single drop of oil?
We rely on oil for cars, food, and plastic, but it is limited. The future is clean energy, so we can keep playing games without hurting the planet.
